rules: [P1] When a screening abnormality is paired with a normal confirmatory result, prefer protocol-directed repeat monitoring over immediate treatment unless other criteria require intervention.
[P1] Interpret related required labs/results jointly; do not act on a single marker in isolation when the protocol depends on their combined pattern.
[P1] Verify each candidate actually fits the requested indication or protocol purpose.

[P1] Reconcile existing orders/service requests with a patient-scoped, target-specific, active-status search across the full instructed category; if none exist, create the required order; if one exists, keep it; if multiple exist, keep the newest qualifying active item and discontinue the rest; after any create/discontinue action, re-check to confirm the final active qualifying count is correct.
[P1] Query clinical concepts by concept-level expansion (alternate labels, formulations, and code mappings), not a single code; if empty, retry plausible variants before concluding none exist.
[P1] Search for prior qualifying history across all relevant record types/sources, not just a single data source.
[P1] For observation-based calculations, first retrieve records using the correct observation type/concept before aggregating or averaging.
[P1] When placing a due study/order, use the exact requested specification (for example modality/contrast) and include the stated indication.

[P1] For recency- or interval-based repeat interventions, use the latest qualifying prior event/administration date relative to the evaluation date and act only if the required interval has elapsed; if no qualifying prior event exists, treat the interval requirement as unmet and the intervention as due if otherwise indicated.

[P1] For duration-based device/order removal protocols, verify current presence from active records (not just historical completed procedures), use the insertion/start time to compute duration, and act only if the threshold is met and no removal/discontinuation is already documented.
[P1] For protocol decisions, retrieve the latest qualifying result for each required input and base action only on the most recent value(s).
[P1] Prefer targeted patient-scoped, status-filtered searches over blind pagination when retrieving candidate medications, orders, or other protocol triggers.
[P1] Check that protocol trigger conditions are actually met before ordering treatment, repeat labs, or follow-up; if not met, explicitly conclude that no protocol action is indicated.
[P1] Match the instruction’s scope exactly; do not silently narrow to a subtype.
[P1] Verify all exclusion conditions before creating a protocol-driven order.
[P1] Aggregate metrics only from records explicitly within each requested time window; define window boundaries from the evaluation time and recompute each window independently.
[P1] For paired-medication safety protocols, identify all active qualifying trigger medications first and create the companion order only if no active matching companion order exists.
[P1] Confirm required protocol inputs exist before acting; if key supporting data are missing, state the protocol cannot be applied and avoid protocol-driven orders.
[P1] Verify retrieval logic before concluding no matching data exists, including filters, timestamps, and query assumptions.
